---
title: "Laszlo Szabo"
subtitle: "Consultant Transplant Surgeon at Cardiff Transplant Unit"
description: "Academic portfolio of Laszlo Szabo, Consultant Transplant Surgeon at Cardiff Transplant Unit. Research in kidney and pancreas transplantation, organ preservation, and clinical data science."
image: images/profile_pic.jpeg
image-alt: "Photo of Laszlo Szabo, Consultant Transplant Surgeon"
format:
  html:
    pagetitle: "Consultant Transplant Surgeon, Cardiff"
    include-in-header: assets/schema-person.html
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rectangle
  image-width: 18em
  links:
    - icon: twitter
      text: X / Twitter
      href: https://x.com/drszabolaszlo
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/drszabolaszlo/
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/Laszlo75
    - text: "{{< fa brands orcid >}} ORCID"
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6881-1542
    - icon: envelope
      text: Contact
      href: mailto:laszlo.szabo@wales.nhs.uk
toc: false
---

I am a Consultant Transplant Surgeon at the **Cardiff Transplant Unit**, specialising in kidney and pancreas transplantation, organ retrieval, organ utilisation, and perioperative outcomes.

My work sits at the intersection of surgery, data, and policy. I lead initiatives to improve access to transplantation and optimise post-transplant care, collaborating with national programmes on organ utilisation policy and contributing to multicentre clinical studies.

## Research Interests

::: {.research-pills}
[Kidney and pancreas transplantation outcomes]{.pill}
[Organ preservation and machine perfusion]{.pill}
[Donor risk stratification and organ utilisation]{.pill}
[Vascular access for haemodialysis]{.pill}
[Data science applied to transplant decision-making]{.pill}
:::

## Current Work

::: {.highlight-block}
I helped establish and now lead the **Normothermic Regional Perfusion (NRP) programme** at Cardiff — improving organ quality assessment and expanding the use of DCD donor organs for transplantation.

I apply R and data science methods to clinical transplant questions, from survival modelling and risk prediction to making better use of national organ offering data. I am also deeply enthusiastic about AI and its potential in medicine, and use **Claude** extensively in my research and analytical workflows.
:::

<!-- ## FAQ

### What is normothermic regional perfusion (NRP)?

Normothermic regional perfusion is a technique used after circulatory death (DCD) donation in which the donor's abdominal circulation is restored using an extracorporeal circuit, allowing organs to be assessed and reconditioned at body temperature before retrieval. NRP improves organ quality and viability, expanding the pool of usable organs. Cardiff is one of the UK centres running an established NRP programme.

### What does the Cardiff Transplant Unit do?

The Cardiff Transplant Unit at University Hospital of Wales performs kidney and pancreas transplants, organ retrievals, and vascular access surgery for patients across Wales and beyond. It collaborates with NHS Blood and Transplant (NHSBT) on national organ utilisation and allocation policy.

### What research does Laszlo Szabo lead?

My research focuses on improving transplant outcomes through better organ selection, preservation, and risk stratification. I am Principal Investigator on several multicentre studies, including PITHIA (pre-implantation kidney biopsy), POWAR (wound infection prophylaxis), and the COPE programme (oxygenated machine perfusion). I also work on data science methods applied to national organ offering data. -->
